common.deleteContentReminder: this only removes the on-chain reference — remember to also delete or unpin your hosted content (IPFS/URL) separately

balance.title: deposit into the pool
balance.hint: to vote anonymously, deposit the fixed MONA amount into the shielded pool. later votes prove you hold a deposit without revealing which one — nothing on-chain links your wallet to your votes.
balance.readyHint: wallet + chip ready — deposit to become eligible to vote.
balance.noMona: this wallet does not hold enough MONA for the deposit amount.
balance.privacyNote: privacy: the deposit is a normal wallet transaction, but your votes are never linkable to it or to each other. you can withdraw your MONA at any time with this wallet.
balance.withdrawNote: you can withdraw your MONA at any time from the dashboard.

dev.title: ⚠ work in progress
dev.body: everything here is still in development. data may be wiped and contracts may be redeployed at any time, so anything you create can disappear. do not use real funds. :)

sponsor.title: sponsor pool is empty
sponsor.hint: there aren't enough funds in the sponsor pool to pay for anonymous transactions right now. anyone can top it up by staking into the Sponsor Vault — it pays the gas for everyone's anonymous actions.

tx.chipNotEnrolled: chip not enrolled — connect the chip and enrol first
tx.registeredLate: you registered your balance after this proposal was created — you can only vote on proposals created after you registered. register, then create a new proposal to test voting.
tx.bridgeUnreachable: chip bridge not reachable — open the rezygcki app and turn on the browser bridge
tx.seedFailed: could not read the chip seed — check the device and try again
tx.deviceHint: check the device screen: it may ask for the PIN — unlock it, approve with D1 and try again
tx.actRejected: chip busy or action rejected on the device — press D1 to approve
tx.actTimeout: timed out waiting for D1 — approve on the device and retry
tx.chipError: chip error — check the device and the bridge window
tx.verifyFailed: the bridge could not verify the chip signature — try again
tx.badDigest: invalid request sent to the bridge
tx.circuitMissing: proof circuit missing — reload the app
tx.notEnrolledOnChain: identity not enrolled on-chain — enrol first
tx.rootNotFound: snapshot not indexed yet — try again shortly
tx.chipNotConnected: chip not connected — tap connect first

dashboard.title: my activity
dashboard.connectPrompt: connect your wallet or chip to see your activity
dashboard.salesNote: ship to the decrypted address, then the buyer confirms receipt to release your funds. cancel to refund the buyer if you can't fulfill.
dashboard.noClaims: no reward claims on this kit yet

404.glitch: signal lost — this page doesn't exist

walkthrough.title: anonymous walkthrough
walkthrough.connectPrompt: connect your signer chip (kit 01) to continue. close the desktop signer app first — it holds the serial port. approve with d1 on the device within a minute.
walkthrough.enrolled: this chip is enrolled — you can publish anonymously
walkthrough.notEnrolled: this chip is not enrolled yet
walkthrough.checking: checking enrollment… (needs the identity registry address configured)

steps.title: 1 · build your signer chip
steps.body: make your rezygcki kit to sign anonymously. it holds a private identity that never touches your wallet or the network.

steps.title: 2 · enrol your identity
steps.body: register the chip's identity commitment on-chain. this adds you to the anonymous set — a merkle tree of enrolled signers — without revealing which one you are.

home.publicNftDesc: your kit is minted as an ERC721 NFT owned by your wallet. you manage versions and removal with that wallet, and you are publicly linked to it.
home.anonymousDesc: published with a zero-knowledge proof from your rezygcki chip (kit 01) — no NFT, no wallet link. ownership is a private commitment, and edits or removal need a zk proof. build and enroll the chip first.

kit.transferTagPlaceholder: new owner tag 0x… (32 bytes, from the new owner's chip)
kit.transferAnonHint: or pass ownership to another chip (stays anonymous):

createKit.anonymousHint: anonymous mode requires the rezygcki chip (kit 01) — build and enroll it first.
createKit.jsonHint: copy this content and upload it to a public place you control (e.g. IPFS), where you can remove it anytime. then set its uri.
createKit.uriHint: paste the public uri where you uploaded the content. you keep full control and can remove it anytime.

comments.hostContentHint: host this content (so you can delete it anytime), then paste its uri

kitGrants.title: treeliner grants · {count}
kitGrants.empty: no treeliner grants for this kit yet

kitMarket.title: products · {count}
kitMarket.empty: no products for this kit yet

kitAgents.title: linked agents · {count}
kitAgents.empty: no agents linked to this kit yet

treelinerGrants.heading: treeliner grants · {count}
treelinerGrants.empty: no treeliner grants match these filters

grant.notFound: grant #{id} not found (or the subgraph is not configured yet)
grant.creator: creator · {address}
grant.salesShareHint: when a fabricator links a product to this grant, they set a % of each sale that flows back to the funders, split in proportion to how much each staked. check each linked product's page for its %.
grant.deleteWithFundersWarning: this grant has funders — deleting it wipes its data, stops future sales slices to funders, and every funder gains the right to blacklist you as a creator.
grant.removedBanner: this grant was deleted by its creator. funding is closed and future sales slices no longer flow. anything already earned can still be claimed below.

createGrant.jsonHint: copy this content and upload it to a public place you control (e.g. IPFS), where you can remove it anytime. then set its uri.
createGrant.uriHint: paste the public uri where you uploaded the content. you keep full control and can remove it anytime.
createGrant.publicBody: the grant is created by your connected wallet — you are publicly the creator and the recipient of the funds. funders stake MONA against your budget and share in the kit's product sales.
createGrant.whyNoAnonymousBody: the grant creator receives the funded MONA, so the role needs a wallet to be paid — grants are public for now.

product.notFound: product #{id} not found (or the subgraph is not configured yet)
product.buyEscrowNote: your shipping address is encrypted to the fabricator and held as a commitment. funds sit in escrow until delivery.
product.shippingEncryptedNote: write your address directly here as plain text, your browser encrypts it with the fabricator's key.
product.linkGrantNote: link this product to a grant so a slice of each sale funds it.
product.grantNeedsFunders: this grant has no funders yet — a grant needs at least one funder before a product can be linked to it.
product.deleteBlockedOrders: can't delete while there are open orders — wait until they're all completed or refunded.
product.grantSameKitNote: only grants for kit #{kitId} can be linked
product.grantPctPlaceholder: % of each sale for the grant funders (1–70)
product.agentsNote: link agents that serve kit #{kitId} — the cyber slice ({pct}%) of each sale splits equally between their owners.
product.cyberZeroHint: cyber slice is 0% — edit the product to set it, otherwise linked agents earn nothing.
product.noLinkedAgents: no agents linked — the cyber slice stays with you
product.noKitAgents: no other agents serve this kit yet
product.searchAgentsPlaceholder: search agents by id or name…
product.agentNotServing: no longer serves this kit — won't receive
product.editHint: edit everything — content, price, slice %, quantity
product.deleteNote: delete only works with no open orders

createProduct.noVersionsForKit: no versions for this kit yet
createProduct.optionsHint: fields the buyer must choose (e.g. size, color). the buyer's choice is sent encrypted to you with the address.
createProduct.sponsorSliceHint: must be between 5% and 70% — this is NOT the grant share, it goes to the protocol treasury
createProduct.cyberSlicePlaceholder: 0–30 (% of each sale → linked cyber agents)
createProduct.cyberSliceHint: optional — split equally between the agents you link to this product; 0 = no agent slice
createProduct.confirmWindowPlaceholder: e.g. 15 — days the buyer has to confirm after you mark shipped
createProduct.confirmWindowHint: after shipping + this many days without buyer confirmation, you can claim the funds; min 1 day
createProduct.shippingKeyAuto: shipping encryption key comes from your wallet (one signature at publish) — buyers' addresses are sealed so only you can read them
createProduct.jsonHint: copy this content and upload it to a public place you control (e.g. IPFS), where you can remove it anytime. then set its uri.
createProduct.uriHint: paste the public uri where you uploaded the content. you keep full control and can remove it anytime.
createProduct.offerBody: you list a product built from a kit version. buyers pay in MONA; funds are held in escrow and released on delivery. shipping is encrypted to your shipping key.
createProduct.fundGrantBody: after creating, you can link the product to a treeliner grant on its page — a slice of each sale then flows back to that grant's treeliners.

agent.notFound: agent #{id} not found (or the subgraph is not configured yet)
agent.cyberRewardsHint: for each kit this agent serves, post results. when a fabricator links this agent to a product, its cyber slice of each sale is paid straight to your wallet — no claim needed.
agent.noResultYet: no result posted for this kit yet
agent.resultUriHint: link to the result output — a page or file (ipfs://, https:// or ar://)

createAgent.name: name *
createAgent.kitsServedRequired: kits this agent serves * (at least one)
createAgent.ioPlaceholder: input it expects + result it posts
createAgent.hardwareSpecPlaceholder: e.g. RTX 4090 / edge TPU / any CPU
createAgent.packageContentHint: copy this content and upload it to a public place you control (e.g. IPFS), where you can remove it anytime. then set its uri.
createAgent.contentUriHint: paste the public uri where you uploaded the content. you keep full control and can remove it anytime.
createAgent.openSourceBody: register an agent so anyone can rebuild it: full model + hardware specs, datasets, deps and reproduce steps live at a uri you control.
createAgent.rewardsBody: attach your agent to kits and post results. as a cyberswagman you earn a slice of those kits' product sales (weight set by governance).

govern.governIntro: govern is the council. voting is anonymous — you prove in zero-knowledge that you hold the chip and a MONA deposit in the pool, and each vote spends one nullifier. no address is ever published.
govern.whatGetsProposedBody:
· blacklist a wallet
· change the quorum
· change the voting time
· change the deposit bucket
govern.theCycleBody: anyone proposes → the window opens → vote yes / no → after it closes, anyone executes if yes ≥ quorum and yes > no.
govern.stateRejected: closed — did not pass (not enough yes votes); nothing to execute

proposal.notFound: proposal #{id} not found (or the subgraph is not configured yet)
proposal.voteConfirm: you're about to vote {choice}. this is final — you can vote only once on a proposal and cannot change or undo it. confirm?
proposal.stateRejected: closed — did not pass (not enough yes votes); nothing to execute
proposal.leftBody: voting is anonymous — chip + MONA, proven in zero-knowledge. it passes only with quorum and more yes than no.

createProposal.bucketNote: the fixed MONA amount a voter must deposit to be eligible. only affects proposals created after this passes.
createProposal.windowNote: how long each new proposal stays open for voting. minimum 1 minute. only affects proposals created after this passes.
createProposal.walletBanNote: blacklists this wallet everywhere on dx (creating, publishing, funding, buying). withdrawals, refunds and claims stay open, so no funds get stuck.
createProposal.submitHint: this only chooses who pays the gas and whether your wallet shows on this action. the vote itself is always anonymous — chip only, no wallet.
createProposal.title: title *
createProposal.packageContentHint: copy this and upload it to a public place you control (e.g. IPFS), where you can remove it anytime. then set its uri.
createProposal.contentUriHint: paste the public uri where you uploaded the content. you keep full control and can remove it anytime.
createProposal.leftIntro: anyone can propose — it's a public transaction, no chip and no MONA needed. only the vote is gated.
createProposal.whatEachTypeNeedsBody:
· blacklist wallet → a wallet address
· quorum → a new vote count
· voting time → new minutes
· deposit bucket → a bucket size
createProposal.leftOutro: after it's created, holders vote yes / no anonymously. it passes only with quorum and more yes than no.
